My
Design
Philosophy

Data is the raw material of modern systems — and messy data is a broken machine. I approach every dataset the way an engineer approaches a circuit: precision first, automation where possible, and zero tolerance for errors that compound downstream. Whether I'm cleaning 100K rows for a client or building embedded systems for the future, I believe that attention to structure is what separates work that merely functions from work that scales.

Every dataset is a story waiting to be told — and bad data
is just a problem waiting to be engineered away.

What I Do

01
Data Cleaning
I clean messy Excel and CSV datasets using Python automation — fixing data types, removing duplicates, handling nulls, and delivering audit-ready outputs. Up to 100K rows across service tiers.
02
Data Analysis & Visualization
Beyond cleaning, I generate multi-chart matplotlib dashboards and revenue summaries that turn raw data into actionable insight for accountants, analysts, and bookkeepers.
03
Mechatronics & Embedded Systems
Currently engineering my foundation in C, MATLAB, and embedded hardware — with a long-term vision of autonomous systems, robotics, and integrated physical computing.
